column3: con- (together) + "flu" (flow) = rivers (or ideas) flowing together into one.
con- (together) + "form" = taking the exact same form or shape as the rest of society.
Dumbfounded - you are so thoroughly confused you are left dumbfounded.
Jell-O - it cools down and thickens into a solid mass, just like Jell-O.
con- (with) + "gen" (nature) = sharing the same nature; highly agreeable and friendly.
con- (together) + "greg" (flock) = a flock of people gathering together.
Geometry - just like congruent triangles, everything fits perfectly together in agreement.
Knives - secretly plotting behind someone's back with knives.
con- (together) + "sacr" (sacred) = to officially declare something holy.
con- (together) + "sens" (feel) = everyone in the room feeling the same way; a general agreement.
Sign - you sign the paperwork to formally hand the goods over to someone else's care.
Console - giving a sad gamer a new console to comfort them after a major loss.
con- (together) + "son" (sound) = sounding together in perfect harmony.
Construct - you carefully construct the meaning of a complex sentence in your mind.
con- (together) + "summa" (highest) = brought to the absolute highest, most complete level of skill.
con- (together) + "tempor" (time) = two events happening at the exact same time.
Contender - a boxer who loves to fight and argue.
contra- (against) + "venire" (come) = to come against a direct rule or law.
From Latin contritus (ground to pieces). Your heart is crushed and ground to pieces by the guilt of what you've done.
Confusion - getting hit in the head so hard it leaves a massive bruise and causes confusion.
Nun drum - a nun playing a heavy metal drum kit is a confusing, puzzling situation to figure out.
con- (together) + "venire" (come) = to officially come together for a meeting.
con- (with) + "viv" (life) = full of life, highly festive, and sociable.
con- (together) + "volvere" (roll) = rolled and twisted together so many times it becomes impossibly complicated.
Copy us - the printer made so many copies you now have an abundant supply.
cord (heart) = acting in a warm, polite, and heartfelt way.
coron (crown) = the official ceremony of giving someone the crown.
corp (body) = having a massive, excessively fat body.
cor- (together) + "robor" (strength) = to add strength to a witness's story by providing matching evidence.
cosm (world) + "polit" (citizen) = a citizen of the world; highly sophisticated and well - traveled.
Coop - the angry chickens violently overthrew the farmer to take control of the coop.
Cover it - you want your neighbor's new car so badly you throw a tarp over it to steal it.
Cover it - keeping the military operation completely covered up and secret.
cred (believe) = an excessive readiness to believe things; extreme gullibility.
culp (blame) = fully deserving of the blame.
Named after Cupid, the Roman god of desire. While Cupid is associated with love, the word evolved to mean an extreme, greedy desire for wealth.
Cursor - moving your computer cursor quickly over the text without actually stopping to read it.
Cut - a spoken response so short and rude it feels like you've been cut off.
Cut tail - to cut the tail off a long meeting to shorten it.
Haunting - a ghost haunting your house is an incredibly intimidating and scary problem to deal with.
Dead earth - a dead patch of earth has a severe scarcity and lack of crops.
Da buckle - the buckle on the CEO's belt breaks during a keynote speech, causing a complete public disaster.
de- (down) + "base" = to lower the value, status, or quality of something.
Da botch - you completely botched your life by engaging in extreme, corrupt partying.
De-bunk - kicking your brother out of the top bunk because his claim that he called dibs is entirely false.
Decorate - having excellent manners, perfectly presented like a beautifully decorated room.
Cry - crying out publicly against a policy you hate.
de- (down/remove) + "face" = to ruin the surface or outward appearance of something (like graffiti).
de- (remove) + "fame" = words specifically designed to destroy someone's fame or reputation.
Reference - treating someone with such deep respect that you always use them as a reference.
File - spilling black coffee all over a clean file, completely corrupting and dirtying it.
Theft - a pickpocket must be incredibly quick and skillful to pull off a theft.
de- (off) + "funct" (perform) = no longer functioning; dead or extinct.
Delete - a computer virus that is so harmful it deletes all your hard drive files.
de- (out) + "line" = to trace the exact lines or outline of a plan in detail.
dem (people) + "agog" (leader) = a toxic political leader who manipulates the dark emotions of the people to gain power.
The mark - setting the physical mark on the ground to define a clear boundary.
de- (down) + "mean" = to put someone down and intentionally make them feel inferior.
Murmur - a shy, modest girl who only speaks in a quiet murmur.
de- (completely) + "nigr" (black) = to heavily blacken someone's reputation with insults.
de- (down) + "nuntiare" (speak/announce) = to publicly announce your rejection of someone.
Deprive - a villain completely deprived of any moral goodness.
Appreciate (the opposite) - to heavily express disapproval of something, the exact opposite of appreciating it.
Dairy licked - the abandoned ice cream factory is so ruined that stray dogs have licked all the rusted dairy vats.
The ride - ruthlessly mocking the kid who is too scared to go on the roller coaster ride.
de- (reverse) + "sacr" (sacred) = to deliberately ruin something holy.
Desert - completely dried out and lifeless, exactly like a desert.
de- (completely) + "sol" (alone) = completely left alone, empty, and barren.
Respond - someone so hopelessly depressed they won't even respond to your texts.
Death pot - a cruel, absolute dictator who throws his enemies into a boiling death pot.
Deserted - someone who has been entirely deserted by wealth, leaving them in extreme poverty.
de- (away) + "via" (way) = wandering away from the straight, honest path to be sneaky.
dia- (through) + "phan" (show) = fabric so light and sheer that light shows right through it.
Dictator - a teacher who constantly lectures you on morals like a strict dictator.
dis- (not) + "fid" (trust) = lacking trust in your own abilities; highly shy and unconfident.
dis- (apart) + "fundere" (pour) = poured apart, spreading out thinly over a wide area.
Delay story - the lawyer told a long, pointless delay story just to waste time in court.
min (small) = extremely or unusually small.
Dirt - the slow, mournful song played while throwing dirt on a coffin.
dis- (not) + "affect" = no longer emotionally affected by or loyal to the leader.
dis- (not) + "avow" (swear) = to swear you have absolutely no connection to something.
dis- (un) + "close" = to un - close the vault and reveal a secret.
Discomfort - to make someone feel deeply embarrassed and in a state of discomfort.
dis- (apart) + "cord" (heart) = hearts apart, creating harsh, clashing sounds or opinions.
Cursive - a speech wandering off topic, looping all over the place just like cursive handwriting.
This stain - looking with absolute snobby disgust at "this stain" on your brand new shirt.
Grunt - an angry, dissatisfied worker walking away just grunting under his breath.
dis- (remove) + "heart" = having your courage or spirit entirely taken away.
Spar - to verbally spar with someone just to belittle and insult their worth.
Separate - two things that are so fundamentally different they must be kept totally separate.
dis- (apart) + "pel" (drive) = to drive away a rumor or bad feeling.
dis- (apart) + "spars" (scatter) = to scatter the crowd in different directions.
dis- (not) + "repute" (reputation) = held in extremely low esteem by the public.
Disassemble - to disassemble your true face and hide behind a fake, deceptive one.
dis- (apart) + "semin" (seed) = to scatter information widely, just like a farmer planting seeds.
dis- (apart) + "sent" (feel) = to feel apart from the group; to officially disagree.
Sip it - to sip your drink so slowly over hours that the ice entirely melts and dissipates.
dis- (apart) + "son" (sound) = musical notes that clash aggressively and don't belong together.
dis- (against) + "suadere" (persuade) = to heavily persuade someone not to do something.
dis- (apart) + "tend" (stretch) = to stretch outwards, usually used for medical swelling.
Dizzy - spinning around dizzy, completely paralyzed and unable to make a decision.
Vine - predicting the future by reading the tangled growth of a magic vine.
Bulge - the secret is bulging out of your pockets until you finally crack and reveal it.
doc (teach) = easily taught and completely submissive to handling.
Dog - a dog guarding its bone is stubbornly attached to it, just like someone stubbornly attached to their unproven opinion.
dorm (sleep) = currently sleeping or temporarily inactive (like a volcano).
Sour - having a sour, gloomy, and extremely severe facial expression.
dub (doubt) = a claim filled with hesitation and serious doubt.
du (two) + "plic" (fold) = being two - faced or highly deceitful.
Stress - being put under extreme physical stress or threats to force a confession.
From Latin ebullire (to boil over). You are so enthusiastic and joyful that your energy literally boils over the pot.
Elect - electing to take the best individual pieces from a wide variety of different styles.
e- (out) + "dict" (speak) = an official, undeniable order spoken out by a king or authority.
ex- (out) + "face" = to completely wipe out or erase the face of a monument.
From Latin effervescere (to begin to boil). Used literally for fizzy drinks, and figuratively for bubbly, highly enthusiastic personalities.
Effective - highly successful in producing the exact desired result.
Front - having the brazen, arrogant nerve to get right up in someone's "front" (face).
Full gem - shining radiantly and brightly like a flawless, full gem.
From Latin ex (out) + grege (flock). Standing out from the rest of the flock in a shockingly bad or horrific way.
Allergy - writing a beautiful, sad poem for a friend who tragically died from a peanut allergy.
Illegal - the detective trying to draw out a confession from someone who did something illegal.
e- (out) + "loqu" (speak) = speaking out in a beautifully fluent, persuasive manner.
e- (out) + "luc" (light) = to shine a spotlight on a complex topic to make it clear.
e- (out) + "ludere" (play) = to play a clever trick to escape the guards.
Mac (Macaroni) - someone who is so starving and bone-thin they desperately need a giant bowl of Mac and Cheese.
Bell - decorating a plain, boring Christmas tree by hanging shiny bells all over it.
Bedazzle - the corrupt accountant stole company funds just to bedazzle her own wardrobe.
Amend - to make precise corrections and improvements to a written text.
Eminem - a highly respected, famous, and prominent figure in their field.
e- (out) + "mollire" (soften) = a lotion designed to completely soften the skin.
em- (in) + "path" (feeling) = physically feeling into someone else's emotions.
Empire - the Roman Empire was built on practical, observable facts and engineering, not just philosophical theories.
Emulator - running a video game emulator on your PC to perfectly imitate an old console.
en- (in) + "amor" (love) = to be entirely filled with a feeling of love and fascination.
Cucumber - trying to run a marathon while carrying a 50-pound sack of cucumbers is a massive burden.
Energy out - a disease that completely drains the energy out of your body.
Franchise - giving a citizen the keys to the "franchise," meaning granting them the legal right to vote.
en- (in) + "gen" (birth) = to give birth to a situation or a feeling of trust.
Enigma machine - highly mysterious and impossible to crack, just like the WWII code machine.
Enemy - the intense, deeply rooted hatred you feel toward a bitter enemy.
Are we? - "Are we there yet?" asked the kid in the backseat, suffering from extreme, soul-crushing boredom.
Originally meant to put someone in "thrall" (slavery). Now it means to capture someone's attention so completely that they are a willing slave to the performance.
From Greek ephemeros (lasting only one day). Originally used by biologists for short - lived insects like mayflies, now means anything fleeting.
Pistol - writing a highly formal series of letters to threaten a duel with a pistol.
Epic tome - an epic, massive tome that serves as the perfect, flawless example of fantasy literature.
equ (equal) + "anim" (mind/spirit) = keeping an even, perfectly balanced mind under intense stress.
equi (equal) + "voc" (voice) = a politician speaking with two equal voices, making their stance completely ambiguous and unclear.
e- (out) + "rud" (raw/rude) = trained completely out of a raw, ignorant state into profound scholarly knowledge.
Ah - choo!-you sneeze, and I deliberately avoid you to escape the germs.
Isolated - knowledge that is so highly isolated that only a tiny group of experts actually understand it.
Spouse - to marry yourself to a political idea or belief, defending it just like you would a spouse.
ether (upper air) = light, airy, and belonging entirely to the heavens rather than earth.
eu- (good) + "pherein" (bear) = bearing a feeling of intense, overwhelmingly good happiness.
e- (out) + "vanescere" (vanish) = fading rapidly out of sight; vanishing like vapor.
Evidence - to clearly show evidence or proof of a hidden feeling.
Extra bad - making a bad situation extra bad by interfering.
Exhale - exhaling in pure awe as you praise and elevate a hero to a high rank.
Ex-aspirin - you have a splitting headache and realize you are out of aspirin, which thoroughly annoys and frustrates you.
ex- (out) + "cav" (cave) = to hollow out a cave or systematically dig historical artifacts out of the ground.
ex- (out) + "culp" (blame) = to officially take the blame off someone's record.
Excrement - a movie so utterly foul and detestable it belongs in the sewer with the excrement.
Exhaust - cheering and urging your team on so intensely from the sidelines that you exhaust yourself.
Exit gent - the building is currently on fire, so it is highly urgent and demanding that the gentleman exits immediately.
ex- (out) + "onus" (burden) = taking the heavy burden of guilt permanently off someone's shoulders.
ex- (out) + "orbit" = a price tag so insanely high it has flown completely out of orbit.
ex- (out) + "ped" (foot) = originally meaning to free a foot from a trap; doing what is most convenient and practical to solve a problem right now.
Expiration - the pirate must do good deeds to make amends for his crimes before his expiration date.
Sponge - using a massive sponge to completely wipe away and erase a criminal record.
Purge - the school board deciding to purge a classic book of all its offensive content before assigning it.
X-tent - the ancient explorer's tent still miraculously stands; it currently exists and survived the storm.
Toll - paying the toll bridge operator with highly enthusiastic praise instead of actual money.
extra (outside) = coming from the outside and therefore entirely irrelevant to the core subject.
Trick - finally figuring out the puzzle's trick to free yourself from the trap.
Vault-pole - vaulting high into the air out of sheer triumph and joy after winning gold.
fac (do/make) = an argument that is so easy to make that it is superficial and entirely lacks depth.
fall (deceive) = based on a false, highly deceitful premise.
Fast tidier - obsessing over every tiny detail; nothing sloppy gets past.
Originally a nautical unit of measurement for water depth (about 6 feet). To fathom a problem is to successfully reach the very bottom of its meaning.
Tattoo us - two drunk, incredibly silly friends shouting "Tattoo us!" at 3 AM; a foolish, pointless idea.
Feces - putting cow feces (manure) on the soil makes it highly fertile and incredibly productive.
Fleece - finding a warm fleece jacket in your trunk right as a blizzard hits is a highly fortunate, well-timed event.
Fur all - a stray animal covered in fur all over, acting completely wild and untamed.
Fever - having a burning, passionate fever for a political cause.
Feet - a swamp smelling as horrific as a rotting pair of unwashed feet.
Feet - putting heavy iron chains on a prisoner's feet to severely restrict them.
Pickle - you can't decide if you want a sweet or a sour pickle because your tastes change constantly and unpredictably.
fid (trust/faith) = strict faithfulness and unwavering loyalty.
Flat - a tire that is completely flat, limp, and entirely lacking firmness.
Flag - a foul on the basketball court so obviously bad and intentional the referee throws a giant penalty flag.
flor (flower) = writing that is heavily decorated and excessively flowery.
Fly out - to openly mock and break the rules, then fly out of the country to avoid arrest.
Aluminum foil - wrapping the villain's evil plans in heavy aluminum foil to completely block and ruin them.
Bear - having the immense patience and self-control not to shoot the bear when it crosses your path.
Before stall - placing a heavy stall in the road before the enemy arrives to entirely prevent their advance.
Torn - your heart is totally torn because you are pitifully sad, lonely, and abandoned.
fort (strong) = immense mental strength and courage while enduring deep pain.
fortun (chance) = something great happening by a lucky, happy accident rather than design.
fract (break) = a group that is easily broken into arguments; highly irritable and unruly.
Caught - a tense situation where you are constantly worried you will be caught because the room is filled with danger.
Frenzy - moving with wild, incredibly fast, frantic energy.
Free volley - playing a completely meaningless, silly free volley in tennis instead of focusing on the actual match.
Fruit girl - the girl who only eats free fruit from the trees to save money because she is highly economical.
Fur thief - the fur thief moves quietly and secretly through the mansion to avoid being caught.
Garnish - piling way too much cheap, brightly colored garnish on a plate, making the whole meal look tacky.
Gary rules - Gary never shuts up about how much he rules; an excessively talkative guy.
gen (nature/born) = having a naturally friendly, warm, and cheerful disposition.
Goat - poking a stubborn goat repeatedly with a sharp stick to provoke it to move.
Gourmet man - a man who loves eating rich, gourmet food in excessive amounts.
